short_description: The challenge for this round is to make a track using the Ami-Sampler only.
long_description: In 1985 the Amiga 1000 featuring the Paula soundchip, supporting 4-channel sample playback, was released, then two years later the Amiga 500, bringing to the masses sample based music creation of an excellent yet affordable facture.  The [Ami-Sampler](https://github.com/astriiddev/Ami-Sampler-VST) is an LV2/VST plugin by astriiddev that aims to bring the sound of the Amiga into your modern music studio.  Even better, it comes with sample packs that, most would agree, define the sound of the Amiga, the ST-01 and ST-02 sample disks from the Ultimate Soundtracker by Karsten Obarski.  Additionally it comes with ST-FV, a selection by astriiddev from other sample disks, and last but not least, ST-ASTRX, a sample pack created by astriiddev.  The challenge of this round is to **make a track using the Ami-Sampler only**.   No other sample can be used, only the ones shipped with the Ami-Sampler.  You can use as many instances of the Ami-Sampler as you wish, as well as any effect and any DAW as long as they are **free and open source**.  Ideally you would attempt to make a track that is in the spirit of the music that was produced on the Amiga at the time but it is ultimately, sountrackerly, up to you.  *Happy music making!*
